 {"id":"gt-fqcz","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: 'Gate timed out'
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Moved from beads
Originally bd-ykqu. Gate notifications are Deacon's job in gastown.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:18.268978-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:18.268978-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-fqcz","depends_on_id":"gt-dh65","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:32.873641-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"gt-frs","title":"Polecat name pooling: Bounded reusable names","description":"Polecats reuse names from a bounded pool (50) with overflow to sequence numbers.

## Naming Scheme
- Pool: polecat-01 through polecat-50 (prefer low numbers)
- Overflow: <rigname>-<sequenceNumber> (e.g., beads-51, gastown-52)

## Design
- Witness tracks which pool names are in use
- On spawn: pick first available from pool
- If pool exhausted: use rigname-N format
- On completion: pool name returns, sequence numbers don't

## Why?
- User experience: tmux sessions survive polecat restarts
- Users stay attached, see new polecat start (like mayor respawn loop)
- Bounded resource usage for common case
- Scales beyond 50 when needed

## Implementation
- Witness maintains name allocation in beads or local state
- Tmux session runs respawn loop (like mayor)
- Name released on graceful exit or when witness detects dead session","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.43866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.551244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:29:30.648439-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-r01","title":"EXTERNAL: Beads Messaging & Knowledge Graph (bd-kwro)","description":"Tracking issue for external dependency on Beads v0.30.2 messaging features.

Beads epic: bd-kwro in ~/src/beads (steveyegge/beads repo)

This blocks GGT work that depends on:
- bd mail send/inbox/read/ack commands
- message issue type
- replies_to threading
- Hooks system for notifications
- Identity configuration

GGT mail commands will be thin wrappers around bd mail once available.

When bd-kwro ships in Beads v0.30.2, close this and unblock dependent work.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:12:02.676883-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T21:35:05.795776-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T21:35:05.795776-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-r6td","title":"gt spawn: Notify Deacon and Witness on polecat start","description":"When gt spawn creates a polecat, it should mail both Deacon and Witness:

```
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'POLECAT_STARTED furiosa' -m 'Issue: gt-xxx'
gt mail send deacon/ -s 'POLECAT_STARTED gastown/furiosa' -m 'Issue: gt-xxx'
```

This enables:
- Witness to bond a lease to its patrol wisp
- Deacon to verify worker started (redundancy)
- Both to nudge if worker is idle at prompt

Part of the village self-monitoring architecture.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:11.790203-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:53:01.103369-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:53:01.103369-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}
